domain_summary: AOF is a self-hosting AI Organization Operating System prototype. This repository evolves the runtime, artifact model, governance surfaces, and bootstrap flow used to operate AOF on itself and on managed projects.
repo_boundaries: docs/ contains the canonical product, release, and model definitions.
src/ contains the runtime, CLI, and verification commands.
schemas/ contains the canonical JSON schemas for AOF artifacts.
.aof/ contains the self-hosting operating memory for this repository.
protected_areas: Release tags and GitHub releases require explicit human approval.
Changes that alter the self-hosting artifact contract should be reviewed before claiming release readiness.
verification_entrypoints: Run npm test for runtime and schema behavior.
Run npm run smoke for end-to-end CLI verification.
Review .aof artifacts when changing self-hosting operating surfaces.
release_constraints: Self-hosting artifacts must stay internally consistent with schemas and runtime expectations.
Human Recognition Interface claims must stay artifact-derived and runtime-backed; decorative UI claims are out of bounds for the current release.
approval_boundary: Schema shape changes, release decisions, and tag creation require explicit human approval. Routine planning and artifact drafting may proceed autonomously.

categories.purpose: Inspect current runtime state, registries, and operator-facing summaries.
Validate integrity, drift, and benchmark-grade compliance.
Write canonical runtime artifacts, decisions, and state transitions.
Advance the runtime, orchestration, or repair path.
Export or visualize evidence, metrics, lineage, and analytics.

top_commands.purpose: Read the command registry so operators and AI can route without loading the full CLI reference.
Verify that bootstrap, orientation, and command registry routing surfaces remain aligned.
Execute a council stage and optionally invoke model-backed seats.
Read the bounded answer-to-proof packet below the operator brief.
Seed a project with the canonical AOF runtime skeleton and recognition packet.
Benchmark whether Need Validation rejects, reframes, and gates project creation correctly.
Read the compact operator-facing brief derived from current runtime situation assessment.
Read the bounded operator progress packet that explains what changed since the last checkpoint.
Read the operator-facing organization summary and active goals.
Verify bootstrap, organization, capability, and command-routing integrity.
Verify that active release refs and governed release surfaces remain aligned.
Start an AOF runtime session from a user request.
Diagnose the current runtime situation, truth conflicts, and best next operating move.
Read the bounded trunk-and-branch position packet for the current frontier.
Upgrade an existing AOF installation to the current bootstrap shape.

runtime_flow: Read the command register first to route without loading the full CLI reference.
Use read commands to inspect runtime state before changing artifacts.
Use verify commands before claiming correctness or release readiness.
Use write commands to persist canonical artifacts and governed state changes.
Use execute commands to move the runtime or orchestration loop forward.
Use observe commands when exporting visibility, metrics, or analytical outputs.
